experimentalTypeTestDescription: Binary-choice; initial preference then model female staged with non-preferred male; scored as preference reversal
effectSizeEstimateType: Count data of preference reversals (copying opportunity vs no-model baseline)
effectSizeEstimateExtra: Exp2 vs Exp1: G=5.65, df=1, p<0.025
effectSizeEstimateNotes: Copying = Exp2 (model contradicts choice) 13/24 vs Exp1 (no-model baseline) 5/24. Counts read from Fig.2.
descriptionGeneralNote: Authors conclude disruption effect, not copying. 2x2: A/B=Exp2 reversed/not, C/D=Exp1 reversed/not.
compare_note: My cells reproduce Jones THE/1 exactly. Jones also has THE/2 lnOR=-1.335 (Exp1-vs-random baseline; not extracted here). Davies extracted affiliation-TIME, 4 ES (3.1=-0.247, 3.2=-0.243, 3.3=+2.813, 3.4=-2.024); 3.3 & 3.4 not reproducible from paper.

experimentalTypeTestDescription: Model female staged with non-preferred male (no predator); reversal vs no-model baseline (Exp1)
effectSizeEstimateType: Count data of preference reversals (treatment vs no-model baseline)
effectSizeEstimateNotes: Reversals: treatment 12/20 (60%) vs baseline Exp1 5/20 (25%). Identical counts in both predator conditions.
descriptionGeneralNote: Within-subject treated as independent groups in 2x2 (standard simplification).
compare_note: My cells reproduce Jones lnOR 1.504 & var 0.475 exactly. Davies d=1.376 from affiliation-TIME (NS in paper, not reproducible from counts; N=40 mis-treats 20 paired females as 2 groups).

experimentalTypeTestDescription: Model female staged with non-preferred male (+ predator); reversal vs no-model baseline (Exp1)
effectSizeEstimateType: Count data of preference reversals (treatment vs no-model baseline)

compare_note: My cells reproduce Jones lnOR 1.504 & var 0.475 exactly. Davies d=1.376 from affiliation-TIME (NS in paper, not reproducible from counts; N=40 mis-treats 20 paired females as 2 groups).

experimentalTypeTestDescription: Focal female chooses male previously adjacent to model female; compared against naive control group (Exp4=11/20)
effectSizeEstimateType: Count data of preference reversals (copying test vs naive control group, Exp4=11/20)
effectSizeEstimateNotes: Treatment 17/20 chose model-associated male vs naive control 11/20. 2x2: A/B=treatment chose-model/not, C/D=control chose-model/not. Total N=40 (20 treatment + 20 control). Single-author paper (mis-cited as Dugatkin & Godin 1992).
descriptionGeneralNote: Paper supports 2 copying contrasts (Exp1 main, Exp5 reversal), each vs the naive control (Exp4); Exps 2,3,6 are further method controls.
compare_note: Chosen design (vs naive control) reproduces Jones SEX/2 exactly. Davies 27.1 d=3.732 = 2+sqrt(3), degenerate-2x2 escalc artifact; its N=40 is CONSISTENT with the treatment+control design (only magnitude is wrong).

experimentalTypeTestDescription: Male positions switched before focal choice (reversal test); compared against naive control group (Exp4=11/20)
effectSizeEstimateType: Count data of preference reversals (copying test vs naive control group, Exp4=11/20)
effectSizeEstimateNotes: Treatment 16/20 chose model-associated male vs naive control 11/20. 2x2: A/B=treatment chose-model/not, C/D=control chose-model/not. Total N=40 (20 treatment + 20 control). Single-author paper (mis-cited as Dugatkin & Godin 1992).
descriptionGeneralNote: Paper supports 2 copying contrasts (Exp1 main, Exp5 reversal), each vs the naive control (Exp4); Exps 2,3,6 are further method controls.
compare_note: Chosen design reproduces Jones SEX/1 exactly. Jones THIRD value DUG1992REV/1 lnOR=2.234 does NOT reproduce from any experiment pair -> spurious/over-count, drop it. Davies 27.2 d=0.558 derivation unclear; N=40 design-consistent.

experimentalTypeTestDescription: Young focal female, older model; chooses male previously beside model female
effectSizeEstimateType: Count data (n of 20 choosing model-associated male; 50/50 rule for C/D)
effectSizeEstimateNotes: 17/20 chose model-associated male vs 50% null. N=20 (NOT 40).
descriptionGeneralNote: Two experiments differ by relative model/focal age.
compare_note: My cells reproduce Jones lnOR & var exactly. Davies d not reproducible from counts; N=40 wrong (=20).

experimentalTypeTestDescription: Female observes actor female mate with a male phenotype (observed ORNAMENTED male mate (copying present)), then chooses between novel ornamented vs non-ornamented males
effectSizeSourceDetails: Fig.5 p.1025 (per-arm counts read from bars); Table 6 interaction
effectSizeEstimateType: Count data (n mating with observed phenotype, split by observed phenotype; 50/50 rule for C/D)
effectSizeEstimateExtra: arm: observed ORNAMENTED male mate (copying present)
effectSizeEstimateNotes: SPLIT extraction (per team request). Observed-ornamented ~9/10 mated same phenotype. Counts read from Fig.5 (not tabulated).
descriptionGeneralNote: SPLIT into two observed-phenotype arms (significant phenotype*age interaction, p=0.0097). Davies instead POOLED into 1 ES (15/21 vs 50%, d=0.628). Total exposed-and-mated N=21.
compare_note: Figure-read cells reproduce Jones (arm) lnOR & var exactly. Davies has only ONE pooled ES (d=0.628) covering BOTH arms - no 1:1 Davies counterpart for the split.

experimentalTypeTestDescription: Female observes actor female mate with a male phenotype (observed NON-ornamented male mate (no copying)), then chooses between novel ornamented vs non-ornamented males

effectSizeEstimateExtra: arm: observed NON-ornamented male mate (no copying)

compare_note: Figure-read cells reproduce Jones (arm) lnOR & var exactly. Davies has only ONE pooled ES (d=0.628) covering BOTH arms - no 1:1 Davies counterpart for the split.

experimentalTypeTestDescription: Computer-animated stimuli (FishSim); model female WITH gravid spot; mate-choice reversal M1->M2 vs no-model control
effectSizeEstimateType: Count data of mate-choice reversals (treatment vs no-model control)
effectSizeEstimateNotes: Reversals: treatment 11/15 vs control 2/15. Control="two" (text p.13); Fig.7C plots ~1 (CONFIRM).
descriptionGeneralNote: Paper supports 2 contrasts (spot, no-spot). 2x2: A/B=treatment reversed/not, C/D=control reversed/not.
compare_note: My cells reproduce Jones USI lnOR & var exactly (control=2). Jones ALSO has MAT/1=MAT/2=3.045 (duplicate code; over-count). Davies d inflated by M1 structural zero-cell (escalc artifact).

effectSizeEstimateType: Count data of mate-choice reversals (treatment vs no-model control)

experimentalTypeTestDescription: Initial association preference, then model female placed with least-preferred male; scored as reversal toward model-associated male
effectSizeSourceDetails: Results p.1157 ("15 of 20 retained, five reversed")
effectSizeEstimateType: Count data of preference reversals (copying; 50/50 rule for C/D)
effectSizeEstimateNotes: Of 20 with a post-model preference, 15 RETAINED and 5 REVERSED (copied). A=reversed=5, B=retained=15 -> OR<1, NEGATIVE. Study found NO copying.
descriptionGeneralNote: Critical sign case. OR<1 = copying did not occur.
effectSizeHowCalculated: OR=ad/bc; lnOR=-1.099, Hedges d(lnOR*0.5513)=-0.61, var(lnOR)=0.4667. A=reversed (copied), B=retained -> OR<1, negative lnOR.
compare_note: Jones lnOR=-1.099 matches my estimate & SIGN (var 0.608 differs: different null). Davies d=+1.140 is the large-male SIZE preference (different construct) + WRONG SIGN for copying; N=40 vs 20.
